histogram_plot.caption: Histogram of cashback_amount across 5,630 customers. Orange = mean, purple = median. The gap between them confirms skew direction: mean > median ⇒ right-skewed, mean < median ⇒ left-skewed.

boxplot.caption: Box plot reads as: box = 25th–75th percentile (the middle 50%), line inside box = median, whiskers extend to 1.5 × IQR, dots beyond = outliers. Large vertical separation between the two medians = strong churn predictor. Box overlap that is still high means the feature alone does NOT distinguish the two groups.

mann_whitney_u.narration: Mann-Whitney U = 2,810,031. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). This non-parametric test asks whether churn and retained customers have the same distribution for this feature.

cohens_d.narration: Cohen's d = -0.42 — small effect size. Values are higher in retained customers (negative shift).

outliers.narration: 438 outliers by the IQR rule (7.8% of rows). p99 = 318.74, max = 324.99. Severity: substantial. The long tail is material. Recommended: log1p transform, OR cap at the 99th percentile inside the preprocessing pipeline. Decision deferred to Stage 3.2 (different treatment per model family may be appropriate).

narrative.distribution: Distribution is strongly right-skewed — most values clustered on the low end with a long right-side tail (skewness = +1.15). Mean (177.22) > median (163.28) — confirms the right skew.
narrative.boxplot: Churned median (149.66) is 16.46 lower than retained median (166.12). A weak separator — some shift but substantial overlap between groups.
narrative.separation: Mann-Whitney U = 2,810,031. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). This non-parametric test asks whether churn and retained customers have the same distribution for this feature. Cohen's d = -0.42 — small effect size. Values are higher in retained customers (negative shift).
narrative.outliers: 438 outliers by the IQR rule (7.8% of rows). p99 = 318.74, max = 324.99. Severity: substantial. The long tail is material. Recommended: log1p transform, OR cap at the 99th percentile inside the preprocessing pipeline. Decision deferred to Stage 3.2 (different treatment per model family may be appropriate).

recommendation.transform_reason: moderate skew combined with substantial outlier tail
recommendation.outlier_action: consider p99 cap or log transform in Stage 3.2
recommendation.rationale: Signal: weak (|Cohen's d| = 0.42, p = 2.518e-38). Transform: log1p (optional) — moderate skew combined with substantial outlier tail. Outlier handling: consider p99 cap or log transform in Stage 3.2.
